div1.entryFree: Recreant, adj. originally == retracting one's errors (recredens) and crying for mercy: feeble desire, all r., poor and meek, Lucr. 710. a caitiff r. to my cousin Hereford, R2 I, 2, 53. Hence == cowardly, faithless: hang a calf's-skin on those r. limbs, John III, 1, 129. John III, 1, 129 John III, 1, 129 a r. and most degenerate traitor, R2 I, 1, 144. to be found false and r. I, 3, 106. puff in thy teeth, most r. coward base, H4B V, 3, 96.
